source_jobs.title: Product Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Luminary

Luminary powers how advisors connect their client’s financial assets with generational gifting and philanthropic giving goals.

Some $90 trillion will pass between generations in the decades ahead – and high net worth asset owners say they want their advisors to lead them through this process. However, more than 70% of these clients feel there is a large gap between the services they expect and what they are receiving.

At the heart of this disconnect is the lack of integrated technology systems – making delivery of wealth transfer advice and services costly and cumbersome. Luminary’s collaboration platform brings together wealth, legal, and tax advisors to help them deliver a delightful end to end experience to their clients.

Luminary was founded in 2022 and count many of the largest wealth managers, law firms, accounting firms and trust companies in the country as customers. We are backed by leading venture capital investors, prominent wealth management firms, and several family offices.



The Opportunity

As a Product Lead at Luminary, you will lead a multidisciplinary team to create industry-leading products that power the future of wealth management. You will join a talented group of product managers & designers and will report directly to Luminary’s Chief Product Officer.

We’re looking for someone who is a true problem-solver at heart and has a strong desire to dive into an industry with huge problems and opportunities in an effort to drive transformational change. You are extremely customer-focused, process and detail-driven, and love bringing simplicity to technically complex problems. You thrive in a fast-paced and results-oriented environment and are willing to take risks and experience failure along the way. You are design-oriented, think in terms of KPIs, and enjoy learning about and applying new technologies to solve customer problems in a user-centric way.



What you'll do

 - Work directly with our CPO and executive team to define the vision and strategy for your area of the product and business

 - Deeply understand our customers (RIAs and institutional wealth management firms), their clients (ultra high net worth individuals) and their workflows

 - Partner closely with our design and engineering teams to build intuitive and delightful products for wealth managers, attorneys and asset owners

 - Interact directly with customers and users to unearth pain points and discover new product opportunities

 - Work closely with our internal subject matter experts and advisory board to define detailed specifications for new product features

 - Create and own your team’s success metrics



Qualities we'd be excited to see in an applicant:

 - 6+ years of product management experience, ideally in fintech

 - Experience owning a significant product roadmap from strategic inception to launch, ideally for a complex multi-product platform

 - Approach decision making with a data-informed mindset, with the ability to gather both qualitative and quantitative data, emerge with insights, and come to well-reasoned conclusions

 - Keen communication and writing ability necessary to work collaboratively across functions (product, design, engineering, sales, customer success, marketing)

 - Outstanding UX/UI sensibilities

 - Technical fluency that enables you to creatively explore possibilities and challenge constraints with engineering, with demonstrated ability to earn the respect of talented engineers

 - An enthusiasm for building AI-first products and experiences



Why you’ll love working here…

 - You’ll have a rare opportunity to work alongside our CPO, CEO and CTO and learn from industry and tech leaders with decades of experience who have built and run multiple billion dollar+ companies

 - You’ll be a part of a small and nimble team where your work has measurable impact to the business’s success

 - You're excited to assume a critical role in building foundational product that will define the future

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Luminary

Luminary is an AI-native system of action for wealth transfer, connecting clients' assets with generational gifting and philanthropic goals.

$90 trillion will pass between generations in the decades ahead, yet more than 70% of high net worth clients report a large gap between the services they expect and what they receive.

Luminary solves this by transforming complex estate planning documents into a structured, proprietary data asset that powers agent-driven workflows for tax and wealth transfer services.

Our customers are leaders across the private client ecosystem — top wealth managers, national tax advisory practices, and law firms among the AM Law 100. Our proprietary data and workflow positions us to rapidly expand from wealth transfer advice into large, adjacent markets including trust administration and estate distribution.

Luminary was founded in 2022 and is backed by leading venture capital investors, prominent wealth management firms, and several family offices.



The Opportunity

As a Customer Success Manager at Luminary, you play a key role in engaging & expanding our customer base, as we continue to rapidly scale.

You will partner with our customers to transform their business with Luminary, creating loyal champions, managing the end-to-end customer lifecycle, from onboarding & engagement through renewals, upsells, and advocacy.

Candidates must be excited by working across multiple layers at customer firms - executives, operations and end users - to drive platform engagement and success. This role offers significant exposure to leadership and rapid growth for ambitious, low-ego individuals.



What you'll do

 - Manage and grow a book of business, owning renewals and expansion

 - Onboard new customers with mutual success plans and clear milestones to achieve customer goals and successful rollouts

 - Develop deep platform and trust & estate knowledge to drive customer engagement through training and ongoing support

 - Partner closely with product and engineering to shape roadmap initiatives with customer insights



Qualities we're excited to see:

 - 5-8 years of experience, with at least 3 as a CSM in B2B SaaS and ideally experience in wealth management or wealth tech

 - Familiarity with wealth management or experience working with RIAs, family offices, or trust and estate attorneys — deeply curious about building domain knowledge

 - Strong project management skills that connect tactical work to strategic outcomes

 - The ability to dig into data to find trends and insights that can be translated into customer success

 - High agency, proactive, and action-oriented

 - REAL — Resilient, Empathetic, Accountable, and Low-Ego



Why you’ll love working here

 - Represent a leading-edge vertical AI application defining a new product category that will transform an industry

 - Work alongside our CEO and co-founders — industry and tech leaders with decades of experience

 - Join a GTM team that collaborates on strategy, commercial assets, and execution

 - Thrive in a fast-paced environment where we're constantly shipping based on customer learnings

 - Luminary is a one-of-a-kind opportunity for massive impact at profitable scale — and a ton of fun along the way.



Compensation

The salary range for this role is $130,000 to $165,000. This role is eligible for a performance-based bonus, commissions, and equity. Compensation is based on a number of factors and may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience.



Benefits we offer

We believe firmly that happy, healthy employees will do their best work.

 - 👩🏻‍⚕️ 100% employee health insurance coverage (plus 50% dependent coverage)

 - 🧘🏽‍♀️ Monthly wellness, commuter, and educational stipends

 - 🏖️ 4 weeks PTO

 - 🛥️ 401(k) plan

 - 🍼 Parental leave policy

 - 🏢 NYC office where much of the team works 3+ days a week

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Product
source_jobs.description:
What you’ll do

 - Drive product development: As a member of a small, high-leverage engineering team, you'll have meaningful ownership over the product development process, significantly impacting our product and business growth.

 - Build dynamic, intelligent interfaces: Use leading AI technology and work with experienced product designers to build best-in-class functionality to transform our customers' workflows.

 - Use the best tooling: Use tools like Meticulous, Amp, Honeycomb, Temporal, and PostHog to build and support industry-leading software. If you're not familiar with these tools, we'll help you learn them!

 - Collaborate in-person with leadership: Work closely with our Head of Product and Head of Engineering in our New York office to elevate our product from early traction to industry leadership.

 - Innovate quickly: Iterate rapidly with the Luminary team to build a generational business that democratizes wealth tech. We ship to production 20+ times per day.



Who you are

 - You have 2+ years of software engineering experience at companies with strong engineering practices.

 - Product-focused: You are a frontend-leaning fullstack engineer with a product mindset. You thrive on understanding what our customers are trying to accomplish and in building functionality to make their lives better.

 - Growth mindset: You want to be a great engineer! You enjoy building software and care about the craft of doing so.

 - Autonomous & responsible: You have a strong work ethic and thrive in environments of autonomy and ownership, understanding the broader impact of your decisions on the product architecture. You identify and advocate for systemic improvements as you see them.

 - Technically skilled: While we're not dogmatic about you already having experience with specific technologies, familiarity with the following is beneficial. If you’re not familiar with some of these, you are curious and excited to learn them!
   
   - Cloud-based web applications and complex data models
   
   - Frontend: React, Typescript, GraphQL (Apollo), Playwright
   
   - Backend: Golang, Postgres, GraphQL

 - Excellent communicator: You excel in communicating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Especially in an environment with a number of remote co-workers, you proactively push information to the rest of the team.



Benefits we offer

We’re a very young company, but we believe firmly that happy, healthy employees will do their best work. To that end, we offer:

 - 👩🏻‍⚕️ 100% health insurance coverage

 - 🧘🏽‍♀️ Monthly wellness stipend

 - 20 days per year of PTO, plus holidays

 - NYC office near Bryant Park



Compensation

 - Base salary range of $140k-$180k, plus meaningful equity

 - Actual compensation package is dependent upon many factors, such as experience and skillset

notes: AI-native system of action for wealth transfer, with a small engineering team
